title: Patchwork teddy bear — head reattach and restuff
description: This teddy bear was in quite a sad condition when we received him. His head was detached from his body, one of his eyes, nose and some of his fur was missing. My client asked me to bring this sad fellow back to life, which has a very special meaning to his wife. We started with removing the old stuffing material from the bear and patching up all the little holes in the fabric scattering all over the head and the body. Then we re-attached the eyes and nose with similar ones,…
itemDetails: Vintage patchwork-style teddy bear plush (tan/brown body with irregular cream/white patches on forehead, muzzle, and paws; black plastic safety eyes and nose). Medium-sized seated bear; no brand visible in project photos—typical of 1980s–1990s mass-market or gift-shop plush (Russ, Gund, and similar lines made comparable two-tone patch bears). Sentimental repair for owner’s spouse (head detached, missing eye/nose/fur, worn fabric). Materials: synthetic plush pile, polyester fiberfill, ribbon and red fabric heart added at completion. Market: sentimental value exceeds resale; generic vintage plush unless tags survive.
repairDetails:
This teddy bear was in quite a sad condition when we received him. His head was detached from his body, one of his eyes, nose and some of his fur was missing. My client asked me to bring this sad fellow back to life, which has a very special meaning to his wife. We started with removing the old stuffing material from the bear and patching up all the little holes in the fabric scattering all over the head and the body. Then we re-attached the eyes and nose with similar ones, re-attached the head to the body, and re-stuffed the bear with new stuffing material and added a ribbon around its neck.

Imported from legacy homepage patient story — 2026-05-16.
